name: Ouroboros
description: Specification-first workflow engine that turns a vague idea into a verified codebase through an evolutionary loop: interview, seed, execute, evaluate, evolve.

description.value: Specification-first workflow engine for AI coding agents. Sits between the user and the agent runtime (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Hermes, OpenCode), replacing ad-hoc prompting with a Socratic interview, an immutable seed spec, multi-stage evaluation, and an evolutionary loop that runs until ontology converges.

website.comment: No dedicated marketing site; the GitHub README is the canonical landing page. PyPI mirror at https://pypi.org/project/ouroboros-ai/.

creator.comment: Single-user GitHub account (owner.type = User), not an organization.

pricing-model.comment: MIT-licensed, install via pip or curl shell script; no paid tier.

covered-phases.comment: Interview = discovery, Seed = spec, Double Diamond Discover/Define = architecture (ontology), Design = planning, Deliver = implementation, Evaluate's Mechanical/Semantic stages = testing, Multi-Model Consensus = review, Reflect/Evolve includes auto-retrospective.

methodology-style.comment: Spec-centric (immutable Seed is the central artifact). Multi-agent because of the Nine Minds personas (Socratic Interviewer, Ontologist, Seed Architect, Evaluator, Contrarian, Hacker, Simplifier, Researcher, Architect). Double Diamond = plan-and-execute pattern.

opinionated.comment: Mathematical gates (Ambiguity <= 0.2 to leave interview, Similarity >= 0.95 to converge) refuse out-of-order progression.

ceremony-level.comment: Socratic interview, immutable seed, three-stage evaluation gate, evolutionary loop with convergence detection.

mechanization-level.comment: End-to-end CLI/MCP execution; `ooo ralph` runs the loop persistently across sessions until convergence. Not 'very-high' because it is self-hosted, not a managed cloud platform.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: Socratic interview is interactive by default; the Ambiguity gate forces the user to clarify before a Seed is generated.

approval-gates.comment: The interview/Seed phase requires reaching Ambiguity <= 0.2 before the workflow advances. Implementation, evaluation, and evolution then proceed without further mandatory human approval (Ralph runs unattended).

autonomous-e2e.comment: `ooo ralph` runs the evolutionary loop persistently across session boundaries until convergence is reached.

autonomy-level.comment: Default workflow has an interactive interview phase but, once seeded, executes and evaluates autonomously through Ralph mode.

rollback-support.comment: Event sourcing (SQLAlchemy + aiosqlite) with checkpoints; per-task git worktrees isolate mutating workflows.

supported-agents.comment: First-party runtime guides for Claude Code, Codex CLI, Hermes, and OpenCode. Hermes is mapped to `other` (no dedicated tag).

editor-integrations.comment: Distributed as a Python CLI plus an MCP server; no IDE plugin. The optional Textual TUI dashboard still runs in the terminal.

model-agnostic.comment: Uses a LiteLLM adapter (100+ providers) with a 3-tier PAL Router (Frugal/Standard/Frontier) that auto-escalates and downgrades.

mcp-support.comment: Ships an MCP server registered automatically by the installer; several skills (`evaluate`, `evolve`, `unstuck`, `ralph`) are exposed as MCP tools.

artifact-formats.comment: Seeds and configs are YAML (e.g. `seed.yaml`, `mcp-config.yaml`); skill/agent definitions and runtime guides are Markdown.

artifacts-covered.comment: Seed = spec; Double Diamond Design = plan; AC decomposition = tasks; evaluation pipeline produces test-plan-style verification; ontology schema doubles as a lightweight architecture artifact. PM mode also generates PRDs.

project-layout.value: Project-level seed.yaml plus a local SQLite event store; mutating runs go into per-task git worktrees under a configurable worktree root.

artifacts-in-git.comment: Seeds (`seed.yaml`) are intended to live in the repo; the SQLite event store is local-state and typically not committed.

language-agnostic.comment: Ouroboros itself is Python, but the workflow targets any codebase; the brownfield explorer auto-detects configs across multiple language ecosystems.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Three-stage Evaluator: Mechanical (free, lint/test style) -> Semantic (LLM review) -> Multi-Model Consensus (agent-review). Ambiguity and convergence thresholds are enforced in code, not just in prompts.

strict-phase-ordering.comment: Ambiguity > 0.2 blocks Seed generation; evolution loop refuses to stop until ontology similarity >= 0.95 (or hits the 30-generation hard cap).

artifact-validation.comment: Seed has a typed, immutable schema; ontology has its own OntologySchema; events have a versioned payload schema.

memory-implementation.comment: Event sourcing on SQLite (SQLAlchemy + aiosqlite) with checkpoints; closest tag is `kv-store`. Not vector-db or knowledge-graph; the YAML seed alone does not warrant `markdown-files`.

persistent-memory.comment: Quoted: "the EventStore reconstructs the full lineage, so even if your machine restarts, the serpent picks up where it left off."

project-context-files.comment: `seed.yaml` is a project-scoped context file checked into the repo and consumed by the runtime.

cost-tracking.comment: TUI token tracker emits warnings at 50/80/95% of a configured budget; orchestrator has a sliding-window shared-budget rate limiter across runtime backends.

run-history.comment: Event sourcing means every step is recorded and inspectable; events have a stable payload schema.

telemetry-opt.comment: No external telemetry collection appears to be implemented (the only `telemetry` references in the codebase are in unit-test fixtures), so there is no disclosed opt-in/opt-out control.

custom-templates.comment: Plugin system auto-discovers user-defined skills and agents; skill markdown files and seed YAMLs can be edited or replaced.

hooks.comment: Pluggable runtime backends (`orchestrator.runtime_backend`) and a plugin system that hooks user-supplied skills/agents into the loop's lifecycle.

sub-agents.comment: Nine Minds personas (Socratic Interviewer, Ontologist, Seed Architect, Evaluator, Contrarian, Hacker, Simplifier, Researcher, Architect) plus a plugin system for user-defined agents/skills.

marketplace.comment: Distributed via the Claude Code plugin marketplace (`claude plugin marketplace add Q00/ouroboros`).

install-mechanism.comment: Primary install is a curl|bash script; pip/uv/pipx are documented alternatives, plus the Claude Code plugin marketplace path.

setup-time.comment: Single curl install command auto-detects Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Hermes and registers the MCP server.

self-hosted.comment: Runs entirely locally; no Ouroboros-side cloud component.

requires-account.comment: No Ouroboros account; the user still needs an upstream LLM provider account (e.g. Anthropic, OpenAI), but that is the runtime, not the workflow.

git-worktree-support.comment: Dedicated `core/worktree.py` provisions per-task git worktrees; configurable via `use_worktrees`, `worktree_root`, `worktree_cleanup`, and `worktree_lock_stale_after_minutes`.

documentation-quality.comment: Extensive docs tree: architecture, CLI reference, config reference, events schema, per-runtime guides (Claude Code, Codex, Hermes, OpenCode), and topical guides (seed authoring, evaluation pipeline, evolution loop). README is rich. Not 5 because some skills lack direct CLI equivalents and some flows are documented only inline.

active-maintenance.comment: Latest release v0.29.1 and most recent push both on 2026-04-21 (the research date itself).

example-projects.comment: `docs/examples/` ships an mcp-config.yaml; getting-started walks through a task-CLI build; an interactive `ooo tutorial` is included.

community-channel.comment: GitHub Discussions enabled; no public Discord/Slack/forum advertised.
